channel.title: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?" - Sign up for a Butterfly Flight! (February 2021 Flight) [OPEN COMMANDER SIGNUPS THIS MONTH!]

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
I'm interested in either flight
I mostly want to help giving better critique for people's ideas or articles that haven't been critiqued yet because I have trouble putting what I want to say when I try to critique.
I am looking to learn new skills to help newer and older writers.
I mostly struggle with word choices and grammar.

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded: Sounds good! I look forward to the flight :)

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
Hello Redesert!
Welcome aboard the (currently unnamed) Flight 2, going from February 15th to 28th. I've seen you around, and I must say, I'm a really big fan of your critiques! I can't wait to start working with you. I'll make sure to keep in touch, and I'll reach out with more info about the flight if you're willing to join me.
Welcome aboard once more!
-Sage

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
Hey there!

So, I haven't done critique in quite a lot of time (at least in the Wiki), so I feel I'm pretty rusted because of that. I thought this would be the perfect opportunity to restart as a critter. The main problems I want to improve would be grammar and clinical tone. Either flight is fine for me, but Flight #1 would be more helpful for me.

Thank you for your time and effort!

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
I would like to join Flight #1

The crit I have given has mostly focused on clinical tone, so I would like to join this flight to expand my narrative critique, and the quality of my critique in general.

I will be fine with any commander that I am assigned to, but this may change when the commanders are chosen. If someone applies who I have interacted with before, I will probably prefer them.

Thank you for your consideration.

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
Interested in either flight.

I'm looking to learn new skills (or refine my existing ones), while getting back into a clearer critique schedule.

Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
P.S. "February 15 to February 30" - As far as I know February only has 28 days this year. I think you mean March 1 or 2.

Adjusted to February 28th. Sorry for the confusion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am interested in either flight (as an officer).
I mostly want to improve my crit/details in my crit because I struggle with this sometimes.
I can give greenlights so I don't mind giving up my spot for a newer account that wants to apply.

P.S. "February 15 to February 30" - As far as I know February only has 28 days this year. I think you mean March 1 or 2.

Edit: I just saw that this might have come over passive aggressive which was not my intention, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• I'm interested in either flight.
  • I've provided some people with critique in the past but would really like to work on expanded my skills in doing so.

item.title: Flight Signup Information
item.content:encoded:
I'm interested! How do I sign up to be a flight officer?

Simply create a New Post/post a new comment (don't use "reply") in this thread stating:

  • which Flight you are interested in (Flight 1, 2, or either)
  • a quick sentence about why you would like to be part of the mentoring process
  • optional: the name of the commander(s) you would like to work with, and one sentence why.

If a commander is interested in mentoring you, they will respond to your post.

I'm interested… but I think I want to work on my critique skills first/I didn't get picked this month. What can I do?

  • Get the basics down! Start here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-9804362/so-you-want-to-be-a-concept-reviewer
  • Read! The Guide Hub "critter resources" section: http://www.scp-wiki.net/guide-hub#toc4 has some resources you can use to practice different kinds of critique, as well as improving your reviewer skills overall.
  • Practice! There are always plenty of authors looking for feedback in the forums.

I'm interested in being a commander/mentor! How do I put my name forward for consideration?

Simply send a wikidot message to Zyn stating your interest, and noting which of the four criteria for giving greenlights you fulfill.

item.title: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
Due to the ever-increasing demand for more skilled reviewers to help out in the forums, the Forum Criticism Staff Team has been holding 2-week mentoring sessions to train up-and-coming reviewers. These are called "Flights".1

What does being part of a Flight mean?

  • Flight 1 will run from February 1 to February 15. Flight 2 will run from February 15 to February 28.
  • Mentors (dubbed "commanders") will work with mentees ("officers") in the Writing Help forums to critique concepts and drafts while the officers practice and improve their critique skills.
  • At the start of the Flight, officers will determine their weekly goals for two categories: activity and quality. Activity can be something simple like "crit at least one thread a day", quality something like "be more familiar with matching authors to helpful resources/guides/example articles". Commanders will discuss these goals weekly.
  • Officers will be taught some forum management skills like how to respond (without backseat modding) to a thread that breaks guidelines.
  • If the officers cannot yet greenlight, they may officially recommend concepts to their commander for greenlight consideration.
  • Flight teams can have funny/rule of cool names. Commander discretion.

Who are the commanders this month?

The commanders for Flight 1 are Karablue9482 and TBA.

The commanders for Flight 2 are Pedagon, Dr Sage Evergreen, and TBA.

Notice: for February, we are opening up commander signups to all individuals who currently have greenlighting privileges! If you're interested in learning about being a commander/mentor, please look at this page for more information.

Who can request consideration to be an officer?

Anyone! Newbies and veterans alike are welcome to put their names forth.

What do Flight participants get at the end?

  • For as long as the mentorship system continues, Flight teams will select the featured SCP, tale, and GoI format displayed on the front page of the site. Flight team member names will also be displayed on the front page of the site.2
  • Officers deemed ready by their commanders will be given greenlighting privileges (if they do not have them already), and their names and number of successful Flights will be displayed on the Butterfly Squad Roster.
  • Flight team member names will be displayed on the corresponding month's Site News page. Officers may optionally provide "flight signoff" messages where they briefly describe the experience and any parting words for future reviewer mentees.
  • (remember, provision of the rewards is up to commander discretion!)
Footnotes
1. Prepare for some Rule of Cool terminology, folks.
2. To accommodate six teams, the number of monthly front page features will be doubled.
